director_prompt:
Vertical Aspect Ratio (4:5). A high-fidelity, photographic architectural synthesis of a singular, impossible London cinema structure, reimagined as a "Vertical Neoclassical Möbius Strip." The composition is a gravity-defying, recursive loop of London’s cinematic heritage. 

The centers of the four building photos are IMMUTABLE ANCHORS and must not be moved, resized, or rotated: the red-brick Arthouse Crouch End facade (top left), the ornate Edwardian brick cinema wing (bottom left), the glowing neon Kiln Theatre facade (bottom right), and the rows of velvet cinema seating (top right) are fixed source photography. 

You MUST aggressively blend, melt, and fuse the edges of these anchors into a unified, sophisticated architectural impossible geometry. The warm red brickwork of the Crouch End Arthouse must seamlessly transition into the grey limestone ornament of the Kiln Theatre's "Foresters Hall" crest. The traditional white-framed windows of the bottom-left brick structure should stretch and warp into the structural supports for the velvet seating, which curves upward to form a plush, upholstered ceiling over the entire atrium. 

The style is "Fragmented Heritage"—a sophisticated fusion of Victorian masonry, Edwardian gables, and contemporary glass, where the perspective is subjective. A staircase from the Kiln bar should lead directly into the arched window of the Arthouse. The atmosphere is warm, nocturnal, and prestigious, lit by the internal glow of the theatre interiors and soft street-level amber. 

Avoid all clichés: no film reels, no popcorn, and no generic sci-fi elements. The final output must look like a real, albeit impossible, photographic site in London. Integrated into the architecture, the text "LONDON CINEMA" is subtly engraved into the limestone frieze of the Kiln Theatre, and "26.02.2026 Thursday" is displayed as a subtle, glowing neon sign within one of the upper windows. Treat the central area of each cutout as locked; only transform and synthesize the edge transition zones to create a singular, coherent, and mind-bending architectural masterpiece.
